feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: American passenger on hantavirus-hit cruise feels ‘blindsided’ and ‘misled’ by new quarantine orders
entry.summary: An American passenger who was exposed to the Andes strain of hantavirus on the cruise ship HV Hondius said he feels “blindsided” and “misled” by new orders that require staying at the National Quarantine Center in Nebraska under federal supervision.

entry.title: This U.S. doctor went to Congo to heal others. Then Ebola hit his hospital.
entry.summary: The American missionary doctor who contracted Ebola may have gotten infected while performing surgery on a patient for what he thought at the time was a gall bladder infection.

entry.title: 'I've never been that weak': Hikers hit with norovirus on the Pacific Crest Trail
entry.summary: The source of the norovirus outbreak remains unknown, but sick hikers on the Pacific Crest Trail were observed near a shared water cache last week.

entry.title: For real heart protection, the weekly exercise number climbs far beyond current advice
entry.summary: Adults should aim to do between 560 and 610 minutes a week of moderate to vigorous physical activity to achieve a substantial reduction in the risk of heart attacks and stroke, suggest the findings of an observational study published in the British Journal of…

entry.title: DR Congo World Cup team still planning US trip despite Ebola outbreak
entry.summary: The DRC team cancelled the Kinshasa leg of its pre-World Cup training due to health restrictions.

entry.title: 'Ebola has tortured us': Fear as DR Congo outbreak spreads
entry.summary: Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa but experts fear the actual number may be much higher.

entry.title: VOTE: Do you have confidence in the CDC to contain another pandemic in the US?
entry.summary: If there is another pandemic in the U.S., will you have confidence in the CDC to contain it?

entry.title: Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship Will Sail Again In June (Latest Updates)
entry.summary: The MV Hondius is being disinfected in the Netherlands and will resume its scheduled trips in mid June.

entry.title: Ebola Outbreak Death Toll Rises To 131, WHO ‘Deeply Concerned’ About Spread (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The head of the WHO said he was "deeply concerned by the scale and speed of the epidemic."

entry.title: Surprising drug combo could reshape treatment for suicide risk
entry.summary: A new study suggests a single ketamine infusion followed by low-dose buprenorphine significantly sustained reductions in suicidal ideation in adults with major depressive disorder.

entry.title: A critical window to stop hantavirus is opening. Not all countries are managing exposed travelers the same way
entry.summary: The hantavirus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius arrived at its last stop Monday. Now, the waiting begins.

entry.title: Douglas County adult dies from hantavirus, health officials say there’s no link to cruise ship outbreak
entry.summary: Hantavirus infections caused by the strain found in Colorado usually happen in the spring and summer.

entry.title: American tests positive for Ebola; U.S. to screen travelers at airports
entry.summary: Health experts are growing increasingly concerned about the outbreak, given that cases could have been spreading undetected for weeks.

entry.title: Michigan health officials warn of rising Salmonella outbreaks linked to backyard poultry
entry.summary: Michigan health officials are reporting an increase in Salmonella infections linked to three multistate outbreaks tied to contact with backyard poultry, particularly baby chicks and ducklings.

entry.title: Brain 'Zaps' From Contact Lenses May Help Ease Depression, Mouse Study Shows
entry.summary: Scientists in South Korea have developed experimental contact lenses designed to send electrical signals through the retina and into brain regions linked to mood.

entry.title: The hantavirus outbreak is the warning the world needs to improve pandemic preparedness
entry.summary: While the hantavirus outbreak will likely be eliminated, it highlights persistent gaps in global pandemic prevention, preparedness and response measures.

entry.title: Genetic Maps Target Root Causes of Multiple Sclerosis
entry.summary: A new study compares CPZ and LPC models, creating a genetic roadmap to accelerate myelin regeneration therapies for Multiple Sclerosis.

entry.title: Scientists Bioengineer Photosynthesis in the Eyes to Treat Dry Eye Disease
entry.summary: Plant machinery turns light into anti-inflammatory power inside eye cells.

entry.title: Allegheny County residents warned of increased risk for ticks and Lyme disease
entry.summary: As Allegheny County residents step outside to enjoy near-record heat this week, local health experts are warning about the dangers of Lyme disease after an unusually high number of tick bites reported across the country. In April, the Centers for Disease Cont…

entry.title: Leucovorin Autism Prescriptions Surge 2,000% via Media Hype
entry.summary: A new study shows a 2,000% explosion in off-label leucovorin prescriptions for autism following media coverage and White House statements.

entry.title: A new way to recharge aging muscle stem cells by restoring a key metabolic component
entry.summary: Losing muscle strength is a natural part of aging. At the core of this decline is a drop in the number of muscle stem cells (MuSCs), the specialized cells responsible for maintaining and regenerating muscle tissue throughout our lives. Loss of muscle strength…

entry.title: 'No season anymore': Viruses are running amok in Northern California
entry.summary: Wastewater data shows that RSV has a moderate presence in multiple Northern California regions, even though it's most common from October to March.

entry.title: Argentina’s icy outpost at the end of the world fears the hantavirus will chill tourism
entry.summary: Remote Ushuaia in Argentina, known as the southernmost city on earth, has found itself at the center of a global media storm involving the deadly hantavirus outbreak.

entry.title: Some people lose far more weight than others on GLP-1s like Wegovy. Here’s why.
entry.summary: Emerging research shows there are ‘super-responders’ to GLP-1 drugs, and others who don’t respond at all.

entry.title: Britain gets experimental drug from Japan to bolster hantavirus response
entry.summary: LONDON, May 18 (Reuters) - Britain has received supplies of the antiviral drug favipiravir from Japan as part of its ongoing response to a deadly ‌hantavirus ...

entry.title: Scientists Accidentally Found a Weird New Way to Stop Mosquitoes From Mating
entry.summary: A Yale study found that garlic triggered a taste receptor that made some insects lose interest in mating and laying eggs.

entry.title: Parkinson’s Drug Restores Memories in Alzheimer’s
entry.summary: Restoring dopamine levels with Levodopa reverses memory decline in Alzheimer's disease models.

entry.title: Trump and RFK Jr. Boosted This Unproven Autism Drug. Some Parents Listened
entry.summary: The FDA recently declined to endorse leucovorin for autism, citing a lack of strong evidence for it.

entry.title: Abortion bans lead to worse outcomes for miscarriages
entry.summary: OHSU study finds state-level restrictions result in substandard medical management of miscarriages, the most common complication in early pregnancy.

entry.title: She initially planned to sleep off her symptoms, only to find out later that she would’ve never woken up: Hea
entry.summary: Allison's first instinct was to just take Tylenol and go to sleep, a decision that could’ve cost her life.

entry.title: How safe are GLP-1 drugs, really?
entry.summary: GLP-1 medications have exploded in popularity over the past few years, and it’s easy to see why. They work. People lose significant weight, blood sugar improves, and for the first time in a long time, weight management feels achievable. But anytime a drug goe…

entry.title: People Are Getting A Little Freaked Out After Learning These 31 Wild Facts About The Human Body
entry.summary: "Memories and songs are stored in two different parts of the brain, so people with Alzheimer’s won’t remember their family, but they’ll remember songs."

entry.title: Scientists Found a Remarkable Way to Help Kids Like Vegetables
entry.summary: Trying to persuade kids to eat healthily is a challenge many parents face, and a small new study suggests that pregnancy might be a prime time to influence toddlers' tastes.

entry.title: Race intensifies to lead next wave of obesity drugs as trial results loom
entry.summary: As next-generation GLP-1 contenders rise, key readouts are on the way from Pfizer, Viking Therapeutics and Eli Lilly.

entry.title: Hantavirus-hit liner reaches Rotterdam, crew being quarantined and ship disinfected
entry.summary: By Charlotte Van Campenhout ROTTERDAM, Netherlands, May 18 (Reuters) - A luxury liner at the centre of an outbreak of hantavirus docked at the Dutch port of ...

entry.title: Chicago Is 2nd Worst City For Mosquitoes. How Bad Will This Summer Be?
entry.summary: Orkin Pest Control ranked Chicago No. 2 in the United States in mosquito activity over the past year, though what that means for this summer is “up in the air," some experts said.

entry.title: They Remember Every Moment of Their Lives. Scientists May Have Just Figured Out Why.
entry.summary: The rare people with highly superior autobiographical memory (HSAM) might owe it to how they sleep.

entry.title: The grain debate: Are refined carbohydrates really bad for the heart?
entry.summary: Are refined grains truly bad for your heart, or are they simply guilty by association? A look at the research reveals a more nuanced picture, writes Carrie Dennett.

entry.title: Runny nose? Itchy? Experts say seasonal allergies worsening due to increase in pollen
entry.summary: A group of Canadian researchers say pollen released by plants during reproduction has gone up across Canada in recent years and is exacerbating allergies from hay fever to hives.

entry.title: These are the U.S. states monitoring for hantavirus cases linked to cruise ship at center of outbreak
entry.summary: Officials in Arizona, California, Georgia, Kansas, Maryland, Minnesota, New Jersey, Texas, Virginia and Washington say they are monitoring residents after...

entry.title: metabolic fitness and immune homeostasis in the gut
entry.summary: This study describes that cardiolipin deficiency-induced mitochondrial dysfunction in Treg cells impairs gut–immune tolerance.

entry.title: Column | Head and neck cancers are on the rise. Here’s how to reduce your risk.
entry.summary: A few major factors are driving new cases of head and neck cancers. Luckily, they are things you can control.

entry.title: Where You Live May Affect How Rapidly You Age, Sweeping Study Finds
entry.summary: The agents guiding the shape of your aging journey may not just come from within.

entry.title: Make Sure You’re Eating These Foods By Your 40s If You Want To Live Longer
entry.summary: A handful of key dietary habits could significantly increase your lifespan.

entry.title: People On GLP-1s Say They Suddenly Have This 1 Unexpected Obsession
entry.summary: As food becomes less stimulating, GLP-1 users are gravitating toward this pricey obsession.

entry.title: 4 Ways To Make Your Daily Walk More Effective
entry.summary: Plus, why you should be aiming for at least 5,000 steps a day.

entry.title: Vermont rejected the federal government’s vaccine rollbacks. But doctors are still seeing their impact.
entry.summary: The latest wave of vaccine skepticism puts an added load on an already-strained primary care workforce, clinicians say.

entry.title: Common Cleaning Chemical Could Triple Your Risk of a Dangerous Liver Disease
entry.summary: Scientists are uncovering a possible connection between everyday chemical exposure and serious liver damage.

entry.title: At least six Americans exposed to Ebola in DR Congo, US media report
entry.summary: One of the six Americans believed to have been exposed is experiencing symptoms, according to media reports.

entry.title: Report: World Isn’t Any More Prepared For A Pandemic Than It Was Pre-COVID
entry.summary: An outbreak of a deadly hantavirus aboard a cruise ship has heightened fears about another global pandemic—which experts say the world isn’t ready for.
